refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and specifies what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There is no conjunction or multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to control packet loss
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss within acceptable limits
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol layer, minimizing coupling with other system functions. It allows independent adjustment of error correction parameters without affecting other system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be tuned to meet the 1% packet loss target under operational policies
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time network condition monitoring as a supporting function to enhance adaptive protocol effectiveness and robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication protocol implementation, including adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional network interface for sending and receiving packets, essential for implementing communication protocol.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs status and performance metrics of the communication protocol for monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as protocol logic is mostly self-contained.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol; would require significant diffusion of protocol logic outside.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol implementation would diffuse across modules.
Safety Services may monitor communication but does not implement protocol; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol DP, as it centralizes protocol logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Risk of protocol changes impacting other modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Possible overlap with Teleoperation if adaptive retransmission parameters are influenced by teleoperation commands.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, satisfying the acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if adaptive retransmission parameters are influenced by teleoperation commands.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface, ensuring packet loss ≤ 1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the communication protocol DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces to Network Link and Telemetry modules.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage potential parameter influence and avoid diffusion.
Define explicit protocols or APIs to isolate adaptive retransmission logic within NGHE Solution to prevent diffusion into other modules.
Include monitoring and fallback mechanisms in the NGHE Solution to handle Network Link interface instability as a containment risk.

decision.justification: Although the current FR to DP and DP to IM mappings pass the gates with high containment and clear allocation, the presence of potential diffusion risks related to overlap with the Teleoperation module and the high safety criticality of the system necessitate further decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable more precise definition and isolation of adaptive retransmission parameters, interface contracts, and monitoring mechanisms to mitigate diffusion and containment risks effectively.
next_level_sub_frs: Define adaptive retransmission parameter control to isolate influence from Teleoperation commands
Specify monitoring and fallback mechanisms for Network Link interface stability
Establish interface contracts and protocols between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module's high containment score indicates a well-defined IM boundary
Potential diffusion with Teleoperation module can be managed through clearer interface definitions
open_risks: Overlap of adaptive retransmission parameters influenced by Teleoperation commands
Network Link interface instability impacting containment
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules
Verification data on monitoring and fallback mechanisms effectiveness
